scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Keep the order short and screen-show friendly; simple drink, sugar, and takeaway language matters more than heavy customization.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: Pointing still helps, but short phrases for water, takeaway, and simple menu clarification do more work than long ordering sentences.

scenarios.name: Ride App / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Showing the destination on your phone helps, especially when street names or station names are hard to pronounce clearly.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Use this mostly for price confirmation and item comparison; bargaining language is lower priority for Italy.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: A brief greeting, thanks, and excuse-me phrase cover a large share of everyday Italy travel friction.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: Keep this scenario practical: water, bag, card, and receipt language matters more than browsing-heavy shopping vocabulary.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Keep your passport and booking details ready and prioritize reservation, check-in, checkout, and luggage help language first.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Save your hotel name, a station or landmark, and the full address in your phone so you can ask for help even if maps fail.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: If something goes wrong, start with repair phrases and show booking screens or photos before trying longer explanations.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Keep small talk light; first-visit comments and food compliments are enough for the first authoring wave.

notes: Shared traveler baseline scaffold for Italian, now reprioritized toward Italy travel utility. Fill translation, pronunciation, and audio posture before runtime wiring.
